sections.label: A
sections.title: Multiple Choice Questions
sections.instruction: Choose the correct option. Each question carries 1 mark.

questions.text:
A game of chance consists of spinning an arrow which comes to rest pointing at one of the numbers 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, and these are equally likely outcomes. What is the probability that it will point at a number greater than 2?

A) 3/4
B) 5/8
C) 1/2
D) 7/8
questions.answer: A) 3/4. Explanation: The numbers greater than 2 are 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8. There are 6 favorable outcomes out of 8. Probability = 6/8 = 3/4.

questions.text:
A piggy bank contains hundred 50p coins, fifty ₹ 1 coins, twenty ₹ 2 coins and ten ₹ 5 coins. If it is equally likely that one of the coins will fall out when the bank is turned upside down, what is the probability that the coin will not be a ₹ 5 coin?

A) 1/18
B) 17/18
C) 5/18
D) 9/10
questions.answer: B) 17/18. Explanation: Total number of coins = 100 + 50 + 20 + 10 = 180. Number of ₹ 5 coins = 10. Number of coins other than ₹ 5 = 180 - 10 = 170. Probability = 170/180 = 17/18.

questions.text:
12 defective pens are accidentally mixed with 132 good ones. If one pen is taken out at random from this lot, find the probability that the pen taken out is a good one.

A) 11/12
B) 1/12
C) 11/13
D) 12/13
questions.answer: A) 11/12. Explanation: Total number of pens = 12 + 132 = 144. Number of good pens = 132. Probability = 132/144 = 11/12.

questions.text:
A box contains 90 discs which are numbered from 1 to 90. If one disc is drawn at random from the box, what is the probability that it bears a perfect square number?

A) 1/10
B) 9/10
C) 1/9
D) 3/10
questions.answer: A) 1/10. Explanation: The perfect square numbers between 1 and 90 are 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, 49, 64, and 81 (total 9 numbers). Out of 90 total outcomes, the probability is 9/90 = 1/10.

sections.label: B
sections.title: Short Questions
sections.instruction: Answer the following questions. Each question carries 2 marks.

questions.text: Gopi buys a fish from a shop for his aquarium. The shopkeeper takes out one fish at random from a tank containing 5 male fish and 8 female fish. Find the probability that the fish taken out is a male fish.
questions.answer:
Total number of fish in the tank = 5 (male) + 8 (female) = 13.
Number of male fish (favorable outcomes) = 5.
Probability of getting a male fish = Number of male fish / Total number of fish = 5/13.

questions.text:
A die is thrown once. Find the probability of getting:
(i) a prime number
(ii) a number lying between 2 and 6.
questions.answer:
When a die is thrown, total possible outcomes = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}, which is 6.
(i) Prime numbers are 2, 3, and 5 (3 favorable outcomes). Probability = 3/6 = 1/2.
(ii) Numbers lying between 2 and 6 are 3, 4, and 5 (3 favorable outcomes). Probability = 3/6 = 1/2.

questions.text:
A lot of 20 bulbs contain 4 defective ones. One bulb is drawn at random from the lot.
(i) What is the probability that this bulb is defective?
(ii) Suppose the bulb drawn in (i) is not defective and is not replaced. Now one bulb is drawn at random from the rest. What is the probability that this bulb is not defective?
questions.answer:
(i) Total number of bulbs = 20. Defective bulbs = 4. Probability of drawing a defective bulb = 4/20 = 1/5.
(ii) Since the first bulb drawn was not defective and is not replaced, the remaining total number of bulbs = 19. The number of remaining non-defective bulbs = (20 - 4) - 1 = 15. Probability that this bulb is not defective = 15/19.
